long_description:
Baseline correction removes systematic offsets and trends from spectroscopic measurements, improving data quality and model performance.

Available Methods:

1. Multiplicative Scatter Correction (MSC)
   - Corrects for multiplicative and additive scatter effects
   - Works well for diffuse reflectance and transmittance data
   - Preserves spectral shape while normalizing intensity

2. Standard Normal Variate (SNV)
   - Center-scales each spectrum independently
   - Removes baseline drift and multiplicative effects
   - Suitable for reflectance spectroscopy

3. Moving Average
   - Simple smoothing-based baseline correction
   - Uses configurable window size
   - Good for noisy data with local baselines

Multiway Data Support:
- For 2D data: standard processing (nway_flag=1)
- For 3D+ data: specify dimensionality and direction
- Direction parameter controls along which axis correction is applied

This preprocessing significantly improves model accuracy and interpretability.

layout.name: method
layout.label: Correction Method
layout.tooltip: MSC: Multiplicative Scatter Correction, SNV: Standard Normal Variate, Moving Average: Smoothing-based correction

layout.name: direction
layout.label: Direction (for multiway)
layout.tooltip: Dimension along which to apply correction (-1 for default/last dimension)

layout.name: window_size
layout.label: Window Size (for moving average)
layout.tooltip: Number of points in the moving average window (only used with moving_average method)

layout.name: nway_flag
layout.label: Data Dimensionality
layout.tooltip: Automatically inherited from Load Data function
